w:r.w:t: Angular • ASP.NET Core 8 • Microservices • Ocelot Gateway • SQL Server
JWT • RabbitMQ • Event-Driven Choreography • Docker • Serilog • Google OAuth • Mega.nz

SmartSure is an enterprise-grade, microservices-based insurance management platform that digitizes the complete insurance lifecycle. It enables customers to register, purchase policies, upload documents, and file claims through guided workflows. Admins manage products, verify claims, and generate operational reports.
Angular 17 — CoreModule, SharedModule, AuthModule, CustomerModule, ClaimsModule, AdminModule, ReportsModule
Ocelot API Gateway with Polly for resilience (retry, circuit-breaker)
SQL Server + EF Core 8 Code-First (one DbContext per service)
RabbitMQ (via Docker) — event-driven choreography; services publish and consume events asynchronously
Services react to each other's events via RabbitMQ queues — no central orchestrator; fire-and-forget for notifications, audit logging, and status propagation
Mega.nz SDK — document uploads for claims
SMTP (MailKit) — notifications for policy and claim events
Serilog — structured logging with console + file sinks
IMemoryCache — policy types, products, user lookups
Docker — RabbitMQ; services run locally in dev

All Angular UI traffic flows exclusively through the Ocelot API Gateway. The gateway validates JWT, enforces rate-limiting, and routes to the appropriate microservice. Services communicate asynchronously via RabbitMQ events. Each service owns its isolated SQL Server database.
Event-Driven Choreography Flow — Policy Purchase & Notifications

Services communicate purely through RabbitMQ events — no service calls another directly. Each service publishes an event when something meaningful happens; interested services listen and react independently. This is choreography: decoupled, asynchronous, and resilient. There is no central orchestrator coordinating the flow.
Policy status set to DRAFT in PolicyDb
Auth Service listens → sends welcome email via SMTP
Admin Service listens → writes AuditLog entry
Claims Service listens → updates claim status; Auth Service listens → sends email to customer
Admin Service listens → writes AuditLog entry
Repository Pattern: Controller → Service → Repository → DbContext in every microservice
Dependency Injection: All services, repositories, and infrastructure registered via IServiceCollection
Global Exception Handling: Custom middleware catches all unhandled exceptions, logs via Serilog, returns RFC 7807 ProblemDetails
IMemoryCache: Applied on policy types listing, insurance product catalog, and user role lookups (5-min TTL)
Polly in Ocelot: 3 retries with exponential backoff + circuit breaker (5 failures → 30s open)
Serilog: Structured JSON logging to console + rolling file sink; includes CorrelationId per request
├── SmartSure.Shared/ ← Class library; referenced by all 4 services
│ │ └── ExceptionMiddleware.cs ← Catches all exceptions; logs via Serilog
All endpoints are prefixed by the Ocelot Gateway. Services run on localhost in dev. Swagger UI available at /swagger on each service port.
KPI cards — total policies, claims, revenue
E. Database Design (SQL Server + EF Core Code-First)

Each microservice owns its isolated SQL Server database with its own DbContext and migrations. Cross-service references are stored as plain GUID columns — NO foreign key constraints cross service boundaries. Data consistency is maintained via RabbitMQ events.

1. GUIDs as PKs on all main entity tables (Users, Policies, Claims, etc.). Lookup/junction tables (Roles, UserRoles, InsuranceTypes, etc.) use INT IDENTITY — they are small, static, and never cross service boundaries.
2. No RefreshToken table — JWT is issued on login; the token is stored in the client's localStorage. Stateless on the server side.
3. Cross-service columns are labelled REF (no FK) — plain GUID with no enforced constraint. Consistency via RabbitMQ events.
Owns all identity data: users, roles, and credentials. JWT is stateless — no refresh token table. IMemoryCache is used for user role lookups to avoid repeated DB hits on every authenticated request.
GUID — main entity, referenced across services
Admin can deactivate; invalidates cache on change
Google OAuth 'sub' claim — null if password login
• user_role_{userId} → 10 min TTL — role checked on every JWT-authenticated request via gateway
• user_profile_{userId} → 5 min TTL — full profile object for GET /profile
Cache is invalidated immediately when IsActive changes or profile is updated.

Owns all insurance product definitions, customer policies, type-specific details (vehicle / home), premiums, and payments. UserId is a plain GUID referencing AuthDb — no FK enforced. InsuranceTypes and SubTypes are small lookup tables cached in IMemoryCache.
Small static lookup — INT; fully cached
Small static lookup — INT; fully cached
Max payout — snapshot from product at purchase
JSON — nominee name, sum insured notes, etc.
• insurance_types_all → 60 min TTL — full InsuranceTypes + SubTypes list (rarely changes)
• policy_{policyId} → 5 min TTL — policy detail for GET /policies/{id}
• user_policies_{userId} → 2 min TTL — customer's policy list for dashboard
Cache is invalidated on policy status change or new purchase.
Owns claim lifecycle and all claim-related documents. PolicyId and UserId are plain GUID references — no cross-DB FK. ClaimStatusHistory provides a full audit trail of every status transition.
DRAFT | SUBMITTED | UNDER_REVIEW | APPROVED | REJECTED | CLOSED
Mega.nz share URL for admin preview
• claim_{claimId} → 2 min TTL — claim detail for customer status tracking page
• user_claims_{userId} → 2 min TTL — customer's claims list
Cache is invalidated immediately on any status change.
Owns audit logs and report snapshots. Data is populated by consuming RabbitMQ events from other services — it never writes directly to other DBs. All cross-service IDs are plain GUIDs.
JSON snapshot of entity state at time of action
Serialised report payload for export / display
• admin_dashboard_stats → 2 min TTL — KPI cards (total policies, active claims, revenue)
• report_{reportId} → 10 min TTL — cached report payload to avoid re-running expensive queries
All cross-service references use GUID — no FK constraints enforced across databases:
Set at purchase; user GUID never changes
Set at claim creation via event payload
From JWT UserId claim on admin action
From JWT on every status transition

ExceptionMiddleware lives in SmartSure.Shared — written once, used by all 4 services via project reference. Each service's Program.cs registers it with a single line. Custom exception types also live in Shared so any service can throw them and the middleware catches them uniformly.
public class ExceptionMiddleware(RequestDelegate next, ILogger<ExceptionMiddleware> logger)
// Returns RFC 7807 ProblemDetails — never raw stack traces
// Each service Program.cs — one line, no duplication
Issued by Auth Service on login; contains UserId, Email, Role, Expiry (60 min)
Validated by Ocelot Gateway using shared secret — services trust Gateway headers
Google OAuth: exchange Google ID token → verify → create/fetch AppUser → issue own JWT
Refresh token: stored in client localStorage — stateless on the server, no DB table

Services never call each other directly. Instead, each service publishes domain events to RabbitMQ queues; interested services subscribe and react independently. This keeps services fully decoupled — a consumer going down does not affect the publisher, and messages are retried automatically when the consumer recovers.
Claims Svc → updates status; Auth Svc → emails customer
Registered as a singleton via AddMemoryCache(). All keys follow a consistent naming convention. Cache is always invalidated on write — never serve stale data after a mutation.
Auth Svc — role checked on every authenticated request
Auth Svc — GET /profile; invalidated on profile update
Policy Svc — InsuranceTypes + SubTypes list (rarely changes)
Policy Svc — policy detail; invalidated on status change
Policy Svc — customer dashboard policy list
Claims Svc — claim detail; invalidated on status change
Admin Svc — KPI cards (total policies, claims, revenue)
Customer uploads file via Claims Service POST /documents
MegaNzService streams file to Mega.nz using the MegaApiClient SDK
Returns a MegaNzFileId and share URL stored in ClaimDocuments table
Admin views documents via the share URL during claim review
On claim rejection/close, documents are retained for audit; deletion is admin-only
Every Service class has a corresponding *Tests.cs file
Repositories are mocked with Moq — no real DB hit in tests
Test categories: Happy path, Validation errors, NotFound, Unauthorized
Coverage target: ≥ 90% line coverage enforced via coverlet
G. Status Lifecycles & Interview Topics Summary
Customer fills wizard; not yet paid
ExceptionMiddleware in SmartSure.Shared — one impl, 4 services register it; returns ProblemDetails
Each service: Models → DbContext → Migrations → SSMS verify
Auth Service issues; Ocelot validates; BCrypt for passwords

Event bus for decoupled async flows — policy activated → email; claim submitted → audit log; claim status changed → email + status update. No central orchestrator; each service reacts to events independently
Per-service, with JWT Bearer security definition
All services; JSON structured logs + rolling file sink
RabbitMQ container via docker-compose; services run locally
NUnit + Moq; ≥90% coverage; no real DB in tests
Gateway routing; retry (x3) + circuit breaker per route
All services/repos scoped; DbContext scoped; MemoryCache singleton
Products, dashboard stats, user roles — 2–10 min TTL
MailKit; triggered by RabbitMQ consumer in Auth Service
Auth Service validates Google ID token; maps to AppUser
Claims Service; MegaApiClient SDK; stores handle + URL in DB
